content: Hi guys. I've rebase our work on top of the rust branch. We have dropped our macro in order to use a Vtable as done in file_operations.rs
Great! Just hit the button so CI will run.
There seems to be a problem in the CI when adding llvm repository
Triggered a re-run -- we had that failure some hours ago.
Yep, it is working now

Hey guys, is there anything else we can do for our pull request, or any of your reviews comments that we didn't fix? I checked and I'm pretty sure we got to all of them, but just in case. Anything else you'd like to see in it?
Let me do a quick documentation/comments review...
Note that in general we will be quite a bit busy this week and the following one since we will be preparing the next drop to the LKML.
But if others can give it a look and think the code is OK, we could put it into the patches we will send.
Thanks a lot for the fast response :D And no worries about that, take all the time you need especially if you're busy
Basically we need to decide what we will put in the patches and what not, and finish other higher priority bits (like the alloc work etc.), but please do not feel like we are neglecting your PR!
Even if we do not end up putting it in right now, do not worry -- we will mention you in the credits and it would also go in later.
Oh don't worry about this, we don't want to add more pressure on you guys! If it gets in this week it gets in, if not no big deal :) Thanks for the review! We're currently working on the fixes
Hi guys, sorry for the lack of progress on the PR. We're all super busy with school right now, we're gonna rebase on Saturday if we have the time!
Hi guys, we have rebased our commits
Hey, we will take a look soon -- we are merging the alloc solution these days and a few things we need for the next drop to LKML
I've rebased our work against upstream rust branch. Some workflow are now waiting for approval
Approved the CI

Miguel Ojeda said:

Hey, we will take a look soon -- we are merging the alloc solution these days and a few things we need for the next drop to LKML

Are we still in this situation?

More or less :P We got alloc in and the LKML drop happened, but given the feedback we got, it looks like we might be close to getting into mainline! Thus we would like to focus on the v2 of the patch series (and preparing the talks for September). Let's see if someone can get you a review...

it looks like we might be close to getting into mainline.

Awsome!

Let's see if someone can get you a review

I've squashed our commits and fixed the conflict. The CI needs to be approved
Running!
For some reason some checks are not launching correctly
Yeah, two seemed stuch in GitHub's CI. Do not worry too much -- for reviewing it does not matter that a couple are not there. In any case, I relaunched them.
I might get some time to work on this in the near future. I will rebase this and maybe get in touch with the SPI maintainer to see if there is any interest on their side

I had some time to make it compile again. I can't test it at the moment as I have no SPI device at hand right now. I guess the next step would be to contact SPI maintainers to see if there is even interest in this, right?
Yeah, I would suggest doing that. (If you didn't see them, there are some notes I wrote at https://rust-for-linux.com/contributing#submitting-new-abstractions-and-modules)
